name: Career Aptitude Test
description: Discover your ideal career path based on your natural strengths and preferences

analytical.title: The Analytical Problem-Solver
analytical.description: You thrive in environments where you can analyze complex information and find logical solutions. You have a natural ability to see patterns and solve problems systematically.
analytical.strengths: Problem analysis, strategic thinking, attention to detail
analytical.challenges: May sometimes overlook emotional aspects of decisions
analytical.careers: Data Scientist, Systems Analyst, Research Scientist, Financial Analyst

creative.title: The Creative Innovator
creative.description: You excel at thinking outside the box and generating original ideas. Your creative mind allows you to see unique connections and possibilities others might miss.
creative.challenges: May sometimes struggle with highly structured environments
creative.careers: Graphic Designer, Content Creator, Marketing Specialist, Product Designer

interpersonal.title: The People-Focused Communicator
interpersonal.description: You have exceptional people skills and thrive in social environments. Your ability to understand, connect with, and influence others is your greatest strength.
interpersonal.challenges: May sometimes prioritize people over tasks
interpersonal.careers: Human Resources, Sales Representative, Public Relations, Counselor

practical.title: The Practical Implementer
practical.description: You excel at turning ideas into reality with a hands-on, practical approach. You're reliable, organized, and skilled at executing tasks efficiently.
practical.careers: Project Manager, Operations Specialist, Executive Assistant, Construction Manager

questions.question: When working on a project, you prefer to:
options: Research all aspects thoroughly before beginning
Brainstorm multiple unique approaches to the problem
Discuss ideas with team members to build consensus
Create a practical plan and start implementing it

questions.question: You feel most energized when:
options: Solving a complex problem that requires deep thinking
Coming up with an innovative idea or design
Having meaningful conversations with colleagues
Completing tasks and seeing tangible results

questions.question: Which task at work excites you the most?
options: Conducting detailed research and analysis
Inventing new solutions or products
Engaging in meaningful discussions with peers
Implementing practical solutions that deliver results

questions.question: During your workday, what activity leaves you feeling most accomplished?
options: Breaking down complex data to uncover insights
Brainstorming creative ideas for future projects
Collaborating with colleagues on innovative strategies
Completing tasks efficiently to achieve measurable outcomes

questions.question: When tackling a work challenge, you tend to:
options: Apply logical reasoning to devise a solution
Generate creative approaches that break the mold
Discuss the issue with your team to gain insights
Implement a practical plan to overcome the obstacle

questions.question: Which professional role do you see yourself thriving in?
options: A role focused on data analysis and strategic planning
A creative role in design or innovative development
A position that emphasizes collaboration and mentorship
A job centered on executing projects with tangible outcomes

questions.question: In your ideal work setting, you value:
options: Opportunities to solve intricate problems
A culture that champions innovative thinking
A collaborative environment with open communication
Clear objectives and the ability to see measurable results

questions.question: Your productivity peaks when you are:
options: Immersed in analytical problem-solving tasks
Brainstorming creative and unconventional ideas
Engaging in deep, collaborative discussions
Focusing on practical tasks that yield clear results

questions.question: When receiving recognition at work, you prefer it to be based on:
options: Your analytical and problem-solving achievements
Your creative contributions and innovative ideas
Your ability to foster strong interpersonal connections
Your effectiveness in executing tasks and delivering results

questions.question: Which aspect of your career do you prioritize the most?
options: Engaging in deep analytical work and research
Developing and implementing innovative ideas
Building strong professional relationships
Focusing on practical execution and achieving results
